# hush_elasticsearch_access_privilege (Resource)

Manage Elasticsearch access privileges in the Hush Security platform.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Create an Elasticsearch access privilege
resource "hush_elasticsearch_access_privilege" "example" {
  name        = "read-logs"
  description = "Read access to log indices"

  grant {
    cluster = ["monitor"]

    indices {
      names      = ["logs-*"]
      privileges = ["read", "view_index_metadata"]
    }
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `grant` (Block List, Min: 1, Max: 1) The Elasticsearch grant configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--grant))
- `name` (String) The name of the Elasticsearch access privilege

### Optional

- `description` (String) The description of the Elasticsearch access privilege

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the Elasticsearch access privilege
- `type` (String) The type of access privilege

<a id="nestedblock--grant"></a>
### Nested Schema for `grant`

Optional:

- `cluster` (List of String) The list of cluster-level privileges (e.g., monitor, manage, all)
- `indices` (Block List) The list of index-level privilege definitions (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--grant--indices))

<a id="nestedblock--grant--indices"></a>
### Nested Schema for `grant.indices`

Required:

- `names` (List of String) The list of index name patterns (e.g., "*", "logs-*")
- `privileges` (List of String) The list of index-level privileges (e.g., read, write, all)
